other_observers: The list is a long one, Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, Rachel Hopper, Glenn Walbek, Peter Gent, Joey Kellner, Loch Killpatrick, the finder, Rob Hinds, Steve Stohoviak (sp) the refinder, Jeannie Mitchell, Lisa Edwards, and more.; Rob Hinds found the bird around 2:30 P.M. on 10/19/2012 on the other side of Upper Twin Pond in a similar habitat, bulrushes on the edge of a marsh, suggesting there might be two birds over two days in the area.
specific_location: Upper Twin Pond, Chico Basin Ranch
description: This well-seen sparrow had an orangish face with a dull gray contrasting auricular. The crown had a broad gray central stripe surrounded on each side by a broad orangish supercilium. The nape was unstreaked and gray. The back was brown with strongly contrasting white vertical streaks. The breast and flanks were orange. The breast was finely streaked with dark gray as were the orangy flanks.Behaviors: Skulking in bulrushes, mostly at the edge of a marsh where they had been accidentaly been sprayed and killed during the eradication of the nearby Russian olives. This bird was mostly responding to 23 people trying to see it so its behavior doesn't mean too much.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic
